tickets.title: Make multi-student analysis visibility obvious in Strategy Recommendation
tickets.description: Users think the agent is analyzing students one-by-one but only producing a single plan for a single student because the UI hides most student analyses behind an expand control. Update the Strategy Recommendation UI to clearly indicate that the plan is generated from the full group (e.g., a summary line like "Based on 12 students analyzed" plus a visible list/preview of students). Improve the expand affordance (label, icon, placement) and consider defaulting to expanded when the cohort is small to reduce confusion. Ensure the loading/progress copy matches the actual behavior (group analysis vs sequential individual analysis).

tickets.title: Update strategy rationale to reference cohort-level evidence (not a single student)
tickets.description: The rationale text currently appears to cite only one student's answers, which undermines trust that the recommendation reflects the whole class. Modify rationale generation to include cohort-level signals (e.g., "X students mentioned...", "Top 3 themes..."), while optionally including a few representative anonymized examples from multiple students. Add guardrails to prevent output that only references one student when multiple students are present, and ensure the rationale is consistent with the set of students actually analyzed. Keep the rationale concise and scannable, with links or expandable sections to drill into individual student analyses.

tickets.title: Add strategy explanation panel for all selectable strategies
tickets.description: Currently only the recommended strategy has detailed elaboration, leaving teachers without enough context when they choose another strategy. Add a consistent “Strategy details” panel (or expandable section) that appears for any selected strategy, including a plain-language definition, when to use it, and an example teacher move. Ensure the content structure is consistent across strategies so users can compare quickly. This should be available both in the initial selection UI and after a strategy is selected (e.g., in a side panel or details drawer).

tickets.title: Show rationale and cautions when a non-recommended strategy is selected
tickets.description: When teachers select a non-recommended strategy, provide clear messaging explaining why it is not recommended for the current scenario and what risks/tradeoffs it may introduce. Include a short “Why not recommended here” rationale tied to the scenario signals (e.g., student emotion level, classroom context, objective mismatch). Add a lightweight callout that suggests the recommended strategy as an alternative, without blocking the user from proceeding. Keep the tone supportive and non-judgmental.

tickets.title: Create and wire up a content model for strategy explanations and rationale
tickets.description: Implement a structured data model for each strategy’s explanation content (definition, when to use, example, cautions, and scenario-specific rationale). Store this in a configuration file or CMS-backed source so content can be updated without code changes. Add UI bindings so the strategy selection components can render the correct explanation fields for any strategy. Include fallbacks for missing fields and basic analytics hooks to track which strategy details are viewed and which strategies are selected.

tickets.title: Clarify current content-generation scope in the UI
tickets.description: Users are unsure whether Engage Agent generates only pictures/videos or other lesson components. Add clear in-product messaging in the generation entry points (e.g., Generate modal, empty states, and results page) that explicitly lists what is supported today (e.g., images/videos) and what is not currently supported (e.g., lesson slides, assessments). Include a short “What can Engage Agent create?” link that opens a lightweight help panel/tooltip to reduce repeated questions and set expectations.

tickets.title: Add placeholder options for 'Lesson slides' and 'Assessments' with out-of-scope handling
tickets.description: Teachers are asking for lesson slides and assessments, but the product decision is that assessments are out of scope and slides may be future work. Add non-functional (or gated) options in the content-type selector for “Lesson slides” and “Assessments” that route to an informational screen: “Not available yet” (slides) and “Not supported” (assessments), with brief rationale and a call-to-action to request the feature. Ensure the options are clearly labeled as coming soon/not supported to avoid users thinking the feature is broken.

tickets.title: Implement a 'Request this feature' feedback capture for lesson slides/assessments
tickets.description: To convert interest into actionable demand, add a simple feedback capture flow when users click the unavailable “Lesson slides” or “Assessments” options. Collect at minimum: role (teacher/admin), grade level, subject, and intended use, plus optional free text. Log events and store submissions in the existing analytics/feedback pipeline so PMs can quantify demand and prioritize future roadmap work.

tickets.title: Add first-time user intro modal with Engage Agent goals and example use cases
tickets.description: New teachers don’t know what the Engage Agent is for or how to apply it (e.g., warm-up activities). Add a first-run introduction modal that briefly explains the agent’s purpose, common teacher goals, and 3–5 concrete example scenarios. Include clear CTAs such as “Watch walkthrough”, “Start with a template”, and “Skip”. Ensure the modal can be dismissed and does not block future use once the user opts out.

tickets.title: Embed a demo walkthrough video that autoplays on first launch with replay access
tickets.description: Provide a short demo video walkthrough that pops up the first time a teacher opens Engage Agent, demonstrating core flows and expected outputs. Implement a lightweight video player in the intro modal with fallback behavior (thumbnail + external link) if embedding fails. Add a persistent entry point (e.g., “Help” or “How it works”) to replay the video later. Track a local/user-level flag so the auto-popup happens only once unless reset.

tickets.title: Create a reusable onboarding framework for agent introductions (config-driven)
tickets.description: Chenyu notes this onboarding should apply across agents, not only Engage Agent. Build a shared, config-driven onboarding component that can display agent-specific copy, video URL, and example use cases while reusing the same UI/logic. Support versioning so onboarding can be re-shown after major updates (e.g., when intro content changes). Ensure the Engage Agent uses this framework as the first implementation.

tickets.title: Add regenerate action for generated media content
tickets.description: Teachers need a quick way to iterate when the generated media (e.g., video) is not satisfactory. Add a prominent "Regenerate" button on the media output panel that re-runs generation using the same inputs/config used for the current asset. Show progress/loading state, disable repeated clicks while a job is running, and preserve the previous version so users can compare or revert. Log regenerate events for product analytics and troubleshooting (job id, failure reason).

tickets.title: Enable teacher custom prompt input to tweak media generation
tickets.description: Teachers want to adjust the generated media based on their own feedback rather than only regenerating blindly. Add an optional "Custom instructions" field that is sent along with the existing structured inputs to influence the next generation, with clear helper text (e.g., tone, pacing, key concepts to include/exclude). Store the custom prompt with the generated asset/version history so subsequent regenerations can reuse or edit it. Include basic guardrails such as character limits and content policy validation before submitting generation requests.

tickets.title: Add version history and selection UI for regenerated media
tickets.description: Regeneration will create multiple candidate outputs; teachers need an easy way to keep the best one. Implement lightweight versioning where each regeneration creates a new media version linked to the same activity/project, with timestamps and the associated prompt/instructions. Provide a UI to preview versions, set one as "Active/Publish", and delete unwanted versions. Ensure downstream flows (export/share/assign) always use the active version.

tickets.title: Clarify individual vs whole-class modes in recommendations UI
tickets.description: Users are confused and assume content generation is only for an individual student, even though the product supports both individual and group use. Update the recommendations stage UI to explicitly show two modes (e.g., tabs/segmented control: “Individual student” vs “Whole class/Group”) with clear labels and brief explanatory microcopy. Ensure the default state is obvious and persists across navigation so teachers understand what context they are viewing. Add lightweight visual indicators (icons/chips) on recommendation cards to denote whether an item is individual- or group-oriented.

tickets.title: Add whole-group performance report materials to recommendation stage
tickets.description: Teachers expect student performance reports at the group level (e.g., class overview), not only individualized materials. Add recommendation items and/or a dedicated section for “Whole-class report” that summarizes performance trends (strengths, gaps, common misconceptions) and suggested next steps for instruction. Provide export/share options consistent with existing flows (e.g., downloadable report or printable view) and ensure it’s clearly differentiated from individual student reports. If group reports already exist elsewhere, surface them prominently in recommendations with direct links.

tickets.title: Reorganize student analysis view by strategy groups with drill-down
tickets.description: Teachers find it inefficient to scan an ungrouped list of individual student analyses and would prefer browsing by strategy category (e.g., Analogy). Update the UI so strategy groups are the primary navigation: clicking a strategy chip/tab expands a panel showing only students classified under that strategy. Include per-group counts and maintain a quick way to switch groups (tabs/chips) without losing context. Ensure the experience still supports searching/filtering within a selected group for large classes.

tickets.title: Add per-strategy group summary and description panel
tickets.description: To make grouped views more useful, add a concise description for each strategy group and a short summary of common patterns seen in that group. Display this at the top of the expanded strategy section (or in a side panel) so instructors understand what the strategy means before reviewing students. The summary should be auto-generated from the underlying analysis and kept brief (e.g., 2–4 bullet points) with optional "show more". Include a fallback state when the system has low confidence or insufficient data to summarize.

tickets.title: Provide "why classified" explanations for each student’s strategy assignment
tickets.description: Teachers want transparency into how and why a student was placed into a particular strategy group. Add an explainability affordance (e.g., "Why?" link or info icon) on each student row that reveals the key evidence used for classification (e.g., highlighted excerpt(s) from the student’s response, key signals/features, and a confidence indicator). The explanation should be phrased in teacher-friendly language and avoid exposing internal model jargon. If confidence is low, explicitly label it and offer a prompt to review/reclassify manually if that workflow exists.

tickets.title: Add teacher view to see all student responses on one page
tickets.description: Teachers need a single page where they can view all students’ responses to a prompt at once to quickly compare similarities and differences. Create a dedicated “Responses” view that lists each student (name/identifier + timestamp) with their response, with sorting and basic filtering (e.g., by class/section, submitted/not submitted). Ensure the view updates in near real-time as responses come in and performs well for typical class sizes. Provide clear empty/loading states and handle long responses with truncation + expand.

tickets.title: Add synthesized summary of common themes and difficulties across responses
tickets.description: Teachers also want an at-a-glance understanding of common difficulties without reading every individual response. Add an aggregated insights panel that summarizes key themes, frequent misconceptions, and overall distribution (e.g., common answer patterns) derived from submitted responses. Include a “show supporting examples” interaction that links each theme to a small set of representative student responses. Make it clear that synthesis is generated and may be imperfect, with an easy way to refresh/regenerate.

tickets.title: Enable drill-down from synthesized insights to individual student details
tickets.description: Teachers should be able to start from the synthesis/dashboard and quickly jump to individual students’ full responses for context. Add deep links from each theme/example to a student detail drawer/page showing full response, metadata, and any related conversation history for that activity. Preserve navigation so teachers can return to the aggregate view without losing their filters/sort state. Ensure permissions align with teacher/class access controls.

tickets.title: Add teacher recommendation input to update engagement strategies
tickets.description: Provide a UI flow for teachers to submit recommendations (free-text plus optional structured tags like "preferred tone", "grouping", "scaffolds", "avoid"). When a recommendation is saved, Engage Agent should incorporate it into future strategy generation for the relevant class/student/context. Include basic validation, clear confirmation messaging, and a way to cancel/discard changes. Ensure the recommendation is stored and retrievable so it persists across sessions.

tickets.title: Enable review, edit, and version history for teacher-recommended strategy updates
tickets.description: Create a settings panel where teachers can view current active recommendations, edit them, disable them, or revert to a previous version. Display timestamps and the author (teacher) for each recommendation entry to support accountability. The system should always use the latest enabled version when generating strategies. Provide a lightweight audit trail suitable for school environments.

tickets.title: Apply teacher recommendations as a controllable constraint in strategy generation
tickets.description: Update the strategy-generation pipeline to treat teacher recommendations as an explicit constraint with a clear priority level (e.g., "must follow" vs "prefer"). When recommendations conflict with existing defaults, the generator should follow the configured priority and surface a brief note indicating what was applied. Add guardrails to prevent unsafe or out-of-policy recommendations from being applied. This should be implemented in a way that is testable and can be toggled via feature flag.

tickets.title: Gate 'Create engagement plan' behind 'Analyze 20 students' completion
tickets.description: On the Strategy Recommendation page, keep the Create engagement plan button disabled until the user has successfully completed the 'Analyze 20 students' step. Add clear UI affordances (disabled state + helper text) to explain what’s required to enable the button, and ensure the enablement persists correctly across refresh/navigation when the analysis is already completed. Validate edge cases (analysis in-progress, failed analysis, fewer than 20 students available) and ensure the button state updates reactively when analysis status changes.

tickets.title: Update engagement plan descriptions when non-recommended strategies are selected
tickets.description: When a user selects strategies other than the recommended one, dynamically update the plan description content to reflect the selected strategy set. Ensure the preview/description is always consistent with the current selection (including multi-select, toggling strategies on/off, and reverting back to recommended). If the content is AI-generated, add a lightweight loading state and cache per-selection results to avoid repeated calls while users experiment.

tickets.title: Move teacher annotation earlier in the strategy recommendation flow
tickets.description: Adjust the recommendation flow so teacher annotation is collected earlier (e.g., after analysis results and before final plan creation) rather than as the final step. Update the UI sequence and data model so annotations influence downstream content (strategy recommendations and/or plan generation) where applicable. Ensure backward compatibility for existing flows by mapping older 'final step' annotations into the new earlier step without data loss.

tickets.title: Add content generation controls for language difficulty and output language(s)
tickets.description: Extend the content generation UI to include a language difficulty control (e.g., grade level/CEFR/simple–advanced slider) and an output language selector that supports one or multiple languages. Persist these settings per plan/content item and pass them through the generation API so outputs are created in the requested difficulty and language(s). Update validation and UX to handle multi-language output formatting (separate sections per language) and ensure defaults are sensible (e.g., match class language when available).

tickets.title: Add in-app definitions and differentiation guide for the four strategies
tickets.description: Teachers report that the distinctions among the four strategies are unclear, which risks inconsistent selection and interpretation. Add a dedicated “What are the strategies?” panel (accessible from strategy selection and results screens) that provides formal definitions, key indicators, and short examples/non-examples for each strategy. Include a “How they differ” comparison view (e.g., table) highlighting overlaps vs. decision rules for classification. Ensure the content is consistent across the app and is easy to scan during lesson planning.

tickets.title: Explain the construct and measurement intent in questionnaire UX
tickets.description: Users are unsure whether the observed distribution (e.g., high experience bridging) reflects the intended construct or is an artifact of prompt framing. Add a brief “About this questionnaire” explanation near the student questionnaire setup and in results, clarifying what is being measured, how responses are interpreted, and known limitations. Include a note that distributions can be influenced by prompts and that the tool aims to minimize leading language. This should reduce confusion and align teacher expectations with the instrument design.

tickets.title: Revise student pre-question templates to reduce leading language toward experience bridging
tickets.description: The current questionnaire appears to prompt students to draw on personal experiences, which may bias responses toward experience bridging. Audit and refactor the default pre-question templates and examples to be strategy-neutral (e.g., avoid explicitly asking for personal experience unless the teacher selects that intent). Provide multiple neutral prompt variants and rotate/allow selection to avoid systematically steering responses. Maintain backward compatibility by versioning templates so historical results remain interpretable.

tickets.title: Add a questionnaire bias-check and preview step for teachers
tickets.description: Teachers need a way to validate that their customized questions are not inadvertently leading students toward a specific strategy. Add a “Preview & Bias Check” step that flags potentially leading phrases (e.g., “in your life,” “a time when you…”) and suggests neutral rewrites. The preview should show how students will see the prompt and highlight terms that may cue specific strategies. Provide an override option with a short rationale capture so intentional design choices are preserved.

tickets.title: Implement automated simulation test harness for strategy distribution across prompt variants
tickets.description: To detect confirmation bias and prompt-driven skew, build an internal evaluation tool that generates simulated student responses across multiple prompt variants and runs them through the strategy classifier/scoring pipeline. The harness should output distribution summaries, confusion matrices (if labels exist), and compare across prompt versions to identify systematic shifts (e.g., inflated experience bridging). Integrate this into CI or a lightweight admin dashboard so changes to questionnaire templates/classification logic trigger distribution regression checks. This supports ongoing QA without requiring manual spot checks.

tickets.title: Improve teacher “self-selected strategy” flow with rationale and guidance
tickets.description: The feedback indicates a need for more explanation around teacher-selected strategies and how they should be chosen. Update the strategy selection UI to include a short “when to choose this” description and optionally ask the teacher to select a goal/rationale (e.g., build relevance, surface misconceptions) that maps to the strategy. Use this rationale to tailor recommended question templates and reduce mismatches between intended strategy and prompt wording. Store the selection and rationale for reporting and later review.

tickets.title: Add filtering and sorting controls to the generated materials gallery
tickets.description: Teachers need faster ways to find previously generated items as the gallery grows. Add filter chips/dropdowns for content type (e.g., worksheet/video/quiz), subject/topic, creation date range, and status (draft/final), plus sorting (newest/oldest/name). Update gallery card metadata to display key filterable attributes and ensure filters persist during navigation and refresh. Include empty-state messaging when no results match filters.

tickets.title: Enable teacher-to-student sharing of generated materials via GENIUS integration
tickets.description: Teachers want to distribute generated materials directly to students (and potentially different materials to different students) instead of only downloading locally. Add a “Share” action on gallery items that opens a modal to select class/section and one or more students (with search), compose an optional message, and send. Implement backend integration to GENIUS for roster lookup and content delivery (link or file push), with delivery status (sent/failed) and clear error handling. Log share events for auditing and allow re-send from the item’s share history.

tickets.title: Support per-student assignment and scheduling for shared materials
tickets.description: The workflow described includes generating content before class and having students view it in class, which requires timing and individualized distribution. Extend the share modal to support assigning different materials to different students and optionally scheduling a release time (e.g., “available at class start”). Provide a confirmation summary showing which students receive which items and when. Store assignments so teachers can view/modify/cancel scheduled shares from a “Shared/Assignments” tab in the gallery.

tickets.title: Add color-coded strategy labels across recommendations UI
tickets.description: Strategy labels are hard to distinguish when scanning recommendation content. Introduce a consistent color-coding system for strategy labels (e.g., chip/badge components) and apply it wherever strategy labels appear (list views and detail panels). Ensure colors are accessible (contrast-compliant) and don’t rely on color alone by keeping the text label and optionally adding an icon/pattern. Define a small, fixed palette and mapping rules for strategy categories, with graceful handling when new/unknown strategies appear.

tickets.title: Improve readability of student recommendation details with visual hierarchy
tickets.description: Recommendation details contain dense text, making it time-consuming to read. Update the detail view to include clearer visual structure: section headers, bullet lists for steps, emphasized key actions, and whitespace between blocks. Add quick-scan cues such as highlighted “Key takeaway” and “Next step” blocks at the top of the detail panel. Keep the rendering compatible with existing content formats (plain text/markdown) and ensure the layout works responsively.

tickets.title: Simplify recommendation stage UI with progressive disclosure
tickets.description: Teachers report the recommendation stage shows too much information, making it time-consuming to scan students. Update the recommendation list to default to a compact view (e.g., student name + key signal(s) + recommended action), with an expand/collapse pattern for details. Add basic sorting/filtering to help teachers focus (e.g., by signal type, recency, number of signals) without requiring performance-level screening. Ensure the compact view still preserves access to evidence so recommendations remain explainable when expanded.

tickets.title: Align recommendation strategies to available signal-based analysis (remove performance-level framing)
tickets.description: The current recommendation strategies appear misaligned because they imply performance levels that the product does not actually compute. Audit recommendation copy, labels, and logic to ensure strategies are explicitly derived from available signals only (e.g., participation drops, missed work patterns), and remove/replace any language referencing "performance levels". Where applicable, show a clear mapping from detected signal(s) to the recommended strategy (e.g., 'Signal → Why this recommendation') to make alignment obvious. This should be implemented both in the UI text and in the recommendation generation prompt/rules so output is consistent.

tickets.title: Add student/group personalization inputs for engagement strategy generation
tickets.description: Teachers want engagement strategies tailored to specific students or groups rather than a single generic output. Add a "Personalize for" section where teachers can define one or more target groups (e.g., "ELL students", "advanced", "needs scaffolding", named groups) and optionally add brief learner characteristics (reading level, interests, common misconceptions, IEP/504 accommodations). Update the generation prompt and output structure so strategies are explicitly labeled per group/student and include differentiation notes. Ensure the UI supports saving/reusing groups across sessions/classes to reduce repeated setup.

tickets.title: Clarify the purpose of Engage Agent videos in the UI and exports
tickets.description: A tester assumed the videos were meant to explain science concepts, leading to perceived low value when videos resemble static images. Add concise microcopy near the video component (and in any exported materials) stating the intended purpose: to inspire thinking and spark discussion, not to deliver full content instruction. Include a short "How to use" tooltip or collapsible panel with suggested teacher prompts/questions aligned to the video. This should reduce misinterpretation and better align expectations with the product’s intent.

tickets.title: Provide more controllable video generation options (style, content variation, simulation)
tickets.description: Teachers want more options to change the content of videos/learning materials and potentially generate simulation-like outputs. Add a set of generation controls such as: animation level (static/light/animated), variation type (different scenario, different representation, different phenomenon), and a "simulation" option when supported (e.g., parameter-driven changes). Implement regeneration with selectable constraints so teachers can iterate without redoing the entire flow. Where true simulation isn’t available, fall back to multi-scene animated sequences that show change over time and clearly label the capability.

tickets.title: Add engagement-strategy context to media generation and gallery metadata
tickets.description: Users report that generated media does not reflect the intended engagement strategies because the output is currently just a generic illustration of the experiment. Add explicit strategy selection (or strategy tags) in the generation flow and store it as metadata on each artifact, so users can see which strategy a media item was generated for. Update prompts/inputs so the generator can incorporate strategy-specific visual elements (e.g., “challenge prompt”, “collaboration roles”, “prediction vs. observation framing”) where applicable, and gracefully fall back when strategy-to-visual mapping is not possible. Surface the selected strategy tag on the artifact card and in the artifact detail view.

tickets.title: Reformat Community Gallery to show lesson/topic and ownership at a glance
tickets.description: The Community Gallery currently hides lesson/topic context behind the zoomed view, making it hard to scan artifacts. Update the gallery card layout to display lesson name/topic (and optionally class/unit) directly on each card, plus a clear “Created by me” indicator and/or filter. Add filters/sorting for lesson/topic and “My artifacts” to help users quickly find relevant items. Ensure the metadata is populated for both existing and newly generated artifacts (with a sensible fallback like “Unknown lesson” for older items).

tickets.title: Implement export/download of a complete learning-materials bundle (not per-asset only)
tickets.description: Users want a Save function that downloads the entire set of learning materials as a single package rather than saving each image/video individually. Add an “Export” action that produces a ZIP (or single packaged file) containing all generated assets plus a manifest (JSON) with lesson/topic, strategy, prompts, and timestamps; optionally include an HTML index page that lays out the materials in a printable/shareable format. Keep the existing per-asset download, but clarify labeling (e.g., “Download asset” vs “Export lesson bundle”). Ensure exports are generated server-side for reliability and include stable filenames and folder structure.

tickets.title: Bind media generation prompts to the selected recommended strategy
tickets.description: Media generation sometimes produces outputs that are unrelated to the recommended strategy because the strategy context is not consistently passed into the generation prompt. Ensure the selected strategy (and its key message, audience, tone, and CTA) is always included as structured inputs to the media generation request. Add guardrails so the generator must reference the strategy’s core concept (e.g., required keywords/themes) and reject/redo generations that miss these constraints. This should apply across all media types supported in Engage Agent.

tickets.title: Add a strategy-to-media alignment check and auto-regenerate option
tickets.description: Implement an automatic alignment evaluation step that checks whether generated media matches the recommended strategy (e.g., semantic similarity to strategy summary, required CTA presence, disallowed-topic checks). If the score is below a threshold, show a warning and offer one-click “Regenerate aligned to strategy” using the same strategy inputs and feedback from the checker (what was missing). Log alignment scores and regenerate events for monitoring and iterative prompt tuning. Ensure the UX is lightweight and doesn’t block users who choose to proceed with off-strategy media.

tickets.title: Expose strategy context and controls in the media generation UI
tickets.description: Users need visibility and control to keep media aligned to the recommended strategy. In the media generation panel, display the currently selected strategy and a short “strategy brief” (message, audience, tone, CTA) that will be used for generation. Provide editable fields or quick toggles (tone/CTA) that update the generation inputs without requiring users to re-run the entire strategy step. Make it clear when media was generated from a different strategy selection and allow switching/re-generating from the correct one.

tickets.title: Add “Why recommended” explanations for each suggested strategy
tickets.description: Teachers need to understand the rationale behind each recommended strategy to trust and apply it. Add an expandable “Why this strategy?” section on each recommendation that explains the inputs/signals used (e.g., lesson goal, student needs, constraints) and the intended outcome. Include brief evidence/heuristics language (e.g., “works well when…”) and avoid implying student-facing delivery if it’s teacher-facing content. Ensure the explanation is generated and/or templated consistently and can be edited/copied by the teacher.

tickets.title: Introduce a personalization workflow when multiple strategies are recommended
tickets.description: When multiple strategies are shown, teachers want guidance on selecting and tailoring them to their context. Add a “Personalize for my class” flow that asks 2–4 quick questions (e.g., grade level, time available, student proficiency/ELL, class size, instructional goal) and then reorders/filters strategies and outputs a tailored plan. Provide suggested combinations (“If you choose A, pair with B”) and highlight trade-offs (time vs. depth, whole-class vs. small-group). Persist the personalization settings per class/lesson so teachers don’t re-enter them repeatedly.

tickets.title: Add a teacher-facing “How to use in class” section with clear expectations
tickets.description: The current experience doesn’t clearly communicate that the recommended materials are for teachers to use in instruction, which creates confusion about student interface and benefits. Add a dedicated “How to use this” section that includes: suggested lesson steps, timing, grouping, prerequisites, and differentiation tips. Add expectation-setting copy near videos/materials clarifying whether they are teacher-led, student-facing, or both, and what students will do during the activity. Provide printable/downloadable directions for classroom use.

tickets.title: Provide a lightweight student-view preview or annotated demo for recommended materials
tickets.description: Teachers can’t assess how learning materials benefit students without seeing what the student experience looks like. Add a “Preview student view” mode (or annotated screenshots/video walkthrough) that shows what students would see and do, plus what data/outputs the teacher will get back. If there is no true student interface, provide an explicit “No student app required” note and show an example student artifact (worksheet response, discussion prompt outcomes). Link the preview directly from each recommended material card.

tickets.title: Add video motion controls to adjust animation speed and easing
tickets.description: Users reported that certain animated elements (e.g., droplets) move unrealistically fast in generated videos. Add configurable controls (e.g., speed multiplier and easing presets like linear/ease-in/ease-out) for video animations so motion can be slowed and made more natural. Default values should prioritize realistic motion, with per-scene overrides for specific layers/elements when supported. Ensure changes are previewable before export and persist with the project.

tickets.title: Implement physics-based motion presets for falling elements (water droplets, arrows)
tickets.description: The current approach appears to animate images with simplistic motion, leading to low-quality results for gravity-driven effects. Add a set of physics-inspired animation presets (gravity acceleration, terminal velocity, bounce/impact options) tailored for common falling elements like droplets and icons/arrows. Provide sensible defaults and allow parameter tweaking (gravity strength, randomness, motion blur). This should improve perceived realism without requiring users to manually keyframe complex motion.

tickets.title: Add automated video quality checks to detect and warn about extreme motion speed
tickets.description: Introduce a lightweight validation step that analyzes animation parameters and flags suspiciously high velocities/short durations for small particles (e.g., droplets). When triggered, show an in-editor warning with one-click fixes such as “slow down to recommended” or “apply droplet preset.” This reduces the likelihood of exporting obviously poor-quality videos and guides users toward better settings. Log these events for debugging and future tuning of default parameters.

tickets.title: Improve video generation pipeline to use scripted animations instead of simple image transforms
tickets.description: Chenyu notes the current state is effectively animating the image, which limits realism and control. Implement a script-driven animation pipeline (e.g., timeline with keyframes/curves or programmatic effects) that can render higher-quality motion for elements like droplets. Start with supporting a small set of scripted effects (fall, drift, particle burst) and migrate existing animations behind the scenes. Ensure exported videos remain consistent across platforms and do not significantly increase generation time without user awareness.

tickets.title: Add automated grammar and spell-check pass for generated descriptions
tickets.description: Users are seeing grammar mistakes in generated content descriptions, which reduces trust and usability. Add a post-generation text quality pass that checks spelling/grammar and applies safe corrections before displaying the final description. Provide a lightweight implementation using an internal LLM “proofread” prompt or a grammar library/service, with guardrails to avoid changing meaning (e.g., preserve product names, proper nouns, hashtags, and required tokens). Log original vs corrected text for debugging and allow easy rollback via a feature flag.

tickets.title: Introduce grammar quality checks in CI for prompt/templates and sample outputs
tickets.description: Some grammar errors may stem from prompt templates or reusable snippets that consistently produce flawed phrasing. Add a small set of snapshot tests that run generation on representative inputs and validate outputs against basic grammar/spell rules (or an LLM-based evaluator with deterministic settings). Fail CI (or warn) when issues exceed a threshold, and include links to the offending prompt/template to speed remediation. This helps prevent regressions as prompts are edited.

tickets.title: Add strategy selector to steer generated materials (e.g., cognitive conflict vs experience bridging)
tickets.description: Teachers report that generated materials skew toward a single approach (experience bridging) and are not aligned with intended strategies like cognitive conflict. Add an explicit “Teaching strategy” control in the generation flow with selectable options (at minimum: Cognitive Conflict, Experience Bridging, Curiosity/Prediction, Observation/Explain). Use the selected strategy to condition prompts/templates for images/videos and accompanying text so outputs reflect the pedagogical intent (e.g., contradictions, surprising phenomena, or prediction prompts for cognitive conflict). Ensure the chosen strategy is saved with the generated artifact and visible when revisiting the content.

tickets.title: Extend video generation to support longer durations and segment types (start-only vs full-result)
tickets.description: Six-second videos often can’t show experiment outcomes, limiting their instructional usefulness and preventing strategy-aligned use cases. Add configurable video duration (e.g., 6s/10s/15s) and a “video segment type” option: Start-only (teaser), Full sequence (start→result), or Result-only (observation first). Default behavior should be strategy-aware (e.g., Start-only for cognitive conflict/prediction, Full sequence for observation/explanation), while still allowing manual override. Update backend generation parameters and UI validation to handle longer runtimes while maintaining reasonable generation time limits.

tickets.title: Generate strategy-aligned teacher guidance and student prompts alongside media
tickets.description: Teachers need clarity on how to use generated images/videos to achieve the intended instructional goal (e.g., how to run a cognitive conflict moment). For each generated asset, include a short “How to use in class” block with: suggested teacher script, 2–4 student questions/prompts, and an intended learning move (predict/notice/explain/resolve conflict). Tailor this text to the selected strategy and the chosen video segment type (e.g., if Start-only, include prediction prompts and reveal instructions). Display this guidance in the same panel as the media with copy/export actions.

tickets.title: Make the three-step materials optional and configurable in outputs
tickets.description: Users report that the generated output prescribes teachers to use a rigid three-step set of materials, reducing flexibility. Add a generation setting (e.g., toggle + optional step count or structure selector) that lets users choose between: (a) three-step structure, (b) flexible/unstructured, or (c) a different number of steps. When flexible mode is selected, the agent should produce materials as modular options (pick-and-choose) rather than required sequential steps. Ensure the setting is persisted per session/template and is visible in the output metadata (e.g., “Structure: Flexible”).

tickets.title: Rewrite language in generated plans to avoid prescriptive 'must use' step sequencing
tickets.description: Even when a structured format is helpful, the current wording implies teachers should follow the three steps exactly. Update prompt templates and/or post-processing so the output uses permissive language (e.g., “Option A/B,” “You can choose,” “Suggested sequence”) and explicitly notes that steps can be reordered, skipped, or swapped. Add a short “Customization” section at the top of the plan that explains how to adapt the materials to different classroom contexts. This should apply regardless of the selected structure setting to prevent overly rigid guidance.

tickets.title: Improve warm-up activity copy clarity and coherence checks
tickets.description: Users reported confusing explanatory text in a warm-up activity (e.g., unclear phrasing about forces acting on raindrops). Add an automatic readability/coherence pass for warm-up activity text that flags awkward or ambiguous sentences and prompts the model to rewrite into clearer, grade-appropriate language. Provide an inline “Simplify / Clarify” action for educators to regenerate only the selected sentence/paragraph without changing the rest of the activity. Ensure the rewrite preserves the intended science concept and avoids introducing new concepts not present in the source prompt.

tickets.title: Add semantic guardrails when converting images to video (do not just animate the still)
tickets.description: Users observed that image-to-video outputs can be irrelevant or confusing (e.g., rain example becomes a student drinking rain), suggesting the system is merely animating visuals without respecting the lesson context. When generating video from an image, require a short “scene intent” derived from the activity text and enforce alignment between the activity concept and the generated motion/actions. Add a validation step that blocks or regenerates videos when detected actions/objects conflict with the concept or introduce inappropriate/unrelated behavior. Include a fallback mode that generates a simpler, concept-safe animation (e.g., rain falling with arrows indicating gravity) rather than adding new narrative elements.

tickets.title: Provide educator controls for video relevance (prompting and constrained actions)
tickets.description: Add UI controls in the video generation panel to let educators specify the intended action (e.g., “raindrops falling due to gravity”) and disallow certain actions (e.g., “no drinking/eating,” “no unrelated characters”). Expose a “Keep visuals literal to concept” toggle that biases the generator toward diagrammatic/illustrative motion instead of story-based interpretation. These controls should be lightweight presets so teachers can fix misalignment quickly without writing detailed prompts.

tickets.title: Reorganize individual Student Recommendation layout for readability
tickets.description: Users report that the individual-student "Student Recommendation" section on the recommendation page is hard to read. Redesign the section into a clearer structure (e.g., grouped headings such as Summary, Evidence/Signals, Recommended Actions, and Suggested Resources) with consistent typography, spacing, and scannable bullets. Ensure the content can be quickly understood in under a minute per student and supports long recommendations without becoming a wall of text (collapsible sections and/or "show more" behavior). Update the underlying rendering to support the new structured format while maintaining backward compatibility for existing recommendation payloads.

tickets.title: Add entry point to Student Recommendations from the Teacher Dashboard
tickets.description: The feedback suggests the Student Recommendation experience should be incorporated into the teacher dashboard for easier access. Add a "Student Recommendations" widget or tab within the teacher dashboard that lists students with key recommendation highlights and status (e.g., new/updated since last visit). Clicking a student should deep-link into the reorganized individual recommendation view (or open an in-dashboard detail panel) while preserving navigation context back to the dashboard. Include basic filtering/sorting (e.g., by class, priority, last updated) so teachers can triage recommendations efficiently.

tickets.title: Add 'Teaching Notes' panel that explains intent and usage for each generated material
tickets.description: Some generated assets (e.g., videos) are hard for teachers to interpret and therefore difficult to use in class. Add a per-asset Teaching Notes panel that clearly states: the learning objective, what the viewer should notice, a plain-language summary of what the scene is trying to communicate, and suggested teacher talk track/questions. Include quick bullets for “How to use” (e.g., warm-up, demo, discussion prompt) and estimated time. Generate this automatically alongside the asset and allow teachers to edit it before exporting/assigning.

tickets.title: Add 'Explain this' / 'What does this show?' action on generated assets
tickets.description: Teachers need an on-demand way to clarify confusing content without leaving the flow. Add an action button on each asset (especially video) that triggers an AI explanation tailored to the specific asset: what happens in the clip, what concept it maps to, and why the actions/props were chosen. The response should include a short version (1–2 sentences) and a longer version with classroom prompts, and it should be saved into the Teaching Notes panel for reuse.

tickets.title: Introduce a 'Comprehensibility check' and revision prompts for generated media
tickets.description: Some assets may be semantically unclear (e.g., ambiguous symbolism or missing context), which reduces usability. Add a generation-stage validation step that scores assets for interpretability based on alignment to the stated objective and presence of explicit cues; when the score is low, automatically propose a revised script/storyboard (e.g., add voiceover, on-screen labels, clearer actions) and offer a one-click regenerate option. Display a brief warning like “May be unclear without narration—add labels/voiceover?” to prevent teachers from exporting confusing materials.

tickets.title: Add automatic retry with backoff for content generation failures
tickets.description: Content generation sometimes fails intermittently and is hard to reproduce. Implement automatic retries for recoverable failures (e.g., timeouts, 5xx, transient upstream errors) using exponential backoff with jitter and a capped max attempt count. Ensure retries are idempotent (e.g., via request IDs) to avoid duplicate content creation, and clearly surface final failure to the user only after retries are exhausted. Track retry attempts and outcomes for observability.

tickets.title: Instrument content generation failures with structured logging and alerting
tickets.description: Add structured logs and error telemetry for the content generation pipeline to help diagnose intermittent failures. Capture request correlation ID, user/org identifiers (as allowed), prompt/content metadata hashes (avoid sensitive raw content), model/provider details, latency, error codes, and retry counts. Emit metrics (failure rate, retry rate, timeout rate) and configure alerting thresholds to notify the team when failure rates exceed a baseline. Provide a simple internal dashboard view to quickly triage spikes and identify root causes.

tickets.title: Standardize primary navigation CTA and reduce competing buttons
tickets.description: Users are unsure where to click because multiple buttons with different sizes/styles compete for attention. Define a single primary CTA placement (e.g., a sticky bottom "Next step" bar) and demote secondary actions to consistent secondary/tertiary styles. Update the design system usage so button hierarchy (primary/secondary/link) is consistent across all steps. Audit key screens in the Engage Agent flow and refactor layouts to remove duplicate/competing CTAs.

tickets.title: Gate "Next step" with explicit prerequisites and inline guidance
tickets.description: Currently, users click "Next step" but still must click/select other information, creating a confusing hidden dependency. Add explicit prerequisite validation so "Next step" is disabled until required inputs/selections are completed, with a clear inline message indicating what’s missing. When users attempt to proceed without completing prerequisites, auto-scroll/focus to the missing field and highlight it. Ensure the prerequisite rules are defined per step so behavior is consistent and predictable.

tickets.title: Add step progress and clear in-step instructions for required interactions
tickets.description: Users need clearer guidance about what actions are required before continuing. Add a step header that shows the current step name/number and a brief instruction line (e.g., "Select X to continue"). Mark required fields/actions with consistent visual indicators and microcopy. This reduces trial-and-error clicking and makes the flow’s required interactions obvious.

tickets.title: Add generation metadata and prompt visibility for gallery assets
tickets.description: Users are confused by seemingly nonsensical or unclear generated videos in the gallery (e.g., "experience bridging 0 Hook: Emma's Drop"). Add a details panel for each gallery item that shows the originating prompt/storyboard, selected template, key generation parameters (model, aspect ratio, duration), timestamp, and who/what created it (manual vs automated). Provide a quick way to copy the prompt and open the generation context so users can understand why an asset looks the way it does. This should be accessible from the gallery card (e.g., "View details").

tickets.title: Introduce an 'Unclear/Bad generation' reporting flow in the gallery
tickets.description: When users encounter confusing outputs, they need a fast way to flag them and provide context. Add a "Report" action on gallery items with reasons like: confusing content, irrelevant to prompt, low quality, wrong style, or unsafe. Capture the asset ID, prompt/params, and optional user notes; send to an internal review/logging endpoint for triage. Optionally allow hiding the reported asset from the user’s gallery view after reporting.

tickets.title: Improve gallery item naming to be human-readable and consistent
tickets.description: Confusing titles like "experience bridging 0 Hook: Emma's Drop" make it hard to interpret generated assets. Implement a naming rule that produces concise, readable names based on campaign/product + concept/hook + media type, and avoid internal tokens/indices leaking into titles. If the generation pipeline currently uses internal step names, map them to user-friendly labels and store a separate internal identifier for debugging. Provide an inline rename option for users to override titles.

tickets.title: Add engagement outcomes tracking for recommended materials
tickets.description: Teachers want to see whether Engage Agent’s recommended materials actually improve student engagement, especially for students typically disengaged. Add a lightweight outcomes tracking flow where, after using a recommended material, teachers can rate observed engagement (e.g., Likert scale) and optionally add a quick note. Store these ratings linked to the specific recommendation, class/section, date, and (optional) student group tags. Ensure the prompt is non-intrusive (e.g., appears after lesson completion or next login) and can be dismissed.

tickets.title: Create an engagement dashboard showing before/after trends by class and student group
tickets.description: Provide a dashboard that summarizes engagement outcomes for Engage Agent recommendations over time. Show before/after comparisons (e.g., baseline engagement ratings vs. post-use ratings) and allow filtering by class, date range, content type, and teacher-defined student groups (e.g., “frequently disengaged”). Include simple visualizations (trend line and distribution) and a table of top-performing recommendations. Make the dashboard accessible from the main navigation and from each recommendation’s detail view.

tickets.title: Add ability to tag and segment 'traditionally disengaged' students/groups for reporting
tickets.description: To evaluate impact on disengaged students, add a way for teachers to create and manage student groups/tags (e.g., “disengaged in traditional classroom”). Allow teachers to apply these tags at the student level (via roster import or manual selection) and use them as filters in engagement reports. Ensure privacy controls are respected (e.g., optional anonymized student IDs, role-based access). This segmentation should integrate with the outcomes tracking so results can be compared across groups.

tickets.title: Add terminology and clarity guardrails for generated explanations
tickets.description: Users report confusing phrasing (e.g., “Noah’s rain”) when the intended concept is gravity. Add a generation constraint layer that enforces use of curriculum-appropriate scientific terms and blocks irrelevant proper nouns unless explicitly provided in the lesson content. Include a lightweight post-generation checker to flag/auto-rewrite unclear sentences (e.g., require subject = gravity, verb = pulls/attracts, object = raindrops) before output is shown. Provide a configurable glossary per lesson/unit (e.g., gravity, evaporation, condensation) that the model should prefer.

tickets.title: Introduce an avatar/human-face style control to reduce uncanny visuals
tickets.description: The avatar/human face appearance is sometimes perceived as weird, reducing trust and engagement. Add a style toggle in the media settings (e.g., “Photorealistic faces: Off/On” or “Illustrated characters” default) and route generation to safer presets when teaching content involves people. Apply face-safety filters (avoid distorted facial features) and fall back to non-face alternatives (silhouettes, back-of-head, or no-person scenes) when quality thresholds are not met.

tickets.title: Enforce storyboard consistency between image and generated video
tickets.description: Users see mismatches when transitioning from image to video (e.g., image shows rain lesson context, video shows student drinking water), making the storyline unclear and irrelevant to the learning goal. Implement a storyboard lock that carries forward the key entities, setting, and action from the selected image into the video prompt (e.g., scene = student in rain; action = gravity pulling raindrops down). Add an automatic relevance check that compares video scene captions against the lesson objective and rejects/regenerates clips with off-topic actions. Expose a “Keep scene consistent with this image” option (default on) on the image-to-video step.

tickets.title: Add guided instruction mode for image animation vs. freeform video generation
tickets.description: Notes suggest exploring the difference between simply animating the image and providing more guided instructions. Add a mode selector: (1) “Animate this image” (minimal motion, preserves composition) and (2) “Generate next scene (guided)” where the user/agent supplies explicit beats (who/what/where/action/goal). Ensure the UI clearly explains the tradeoff: higher coherence for animation vs. more flexibility but needs guidance for next-scene generation. Persist the chosen mode per lesson so teachers can standardize output quality.

tickets.title: Add student sentiment/engagement view for lesson materials
tickets.description: Teachers want visibility into how students feel about the material (e.g., confusion, interest, frustration) to adapt instruction. Add a "Student sentiment" module that summarizes class-level sentiment for the current material and highlights top signals (e.g., % confused vs. confident) and notable shifts over time. The module should clearly distinguish between inferred signals (from interactions) vs. explicitly submitted feedback, and provide links to the underlying evidence (e.g., common questions, thumbs up/down counts). Ensure the UI is lightweight and accessible from the material/lesson view without disrupting the primary workflow.

tickets.title: Collect lightweight student reactions tied to specific material sections
tickets.description: To support sentiment reporting, add an option for students to submit quick reactions while viewing/using materials (e.g., 1–5 understanding, "confusing" flag, "interesting" flag, optional short comment). Reactions should be attachable to a specific slide/page/section and timestamped so teachers can pinpoint where sentiment changes. Include basic moderation controls (e.g., hide a comment) and privacy settings (aggregate-only by default; no individual attribution unless explicitly enabled by the institution/teacher settings).

tickets.title: Add AI evaluation for student responses with misconception detection
tickets.description: Teachers want automatic evaluation of student responses to quickly identify difficulties or misconceptions. Add an evaluation capability that ingests a student response (text, and optionally an image/OCR of work) plus the question/prompt and returns: correctness (or rubric level), brief rationale, detected misconceptions, and suggested next-step feedback. The output should be structured (e.g., JSON) to support downstream UI rendering and export. Include guardrails such as confidence indicators and a “needs teacher review” flag when the model is uncertain or the response is ambiguous.

tickets.title: Create a teacher-facing UI panel to review evaluations across students
tickets.description: To save diagnosis time, surface evaluations in a compact review interface rather than requiring teachers to open each response individually. Add an evaluation panel/table view showing each student, their response, evaluation result, confidence, and tagged misconceptions, with filters (e.g., by misconception tag, incorrect/partial). Provide drill-down to see the full reasoning/notes and suggested feedback, and allow teachers to override the evaluation and mark the final status. Ensure the UI clearly communicates AI-generated content and supports quick triage workflows.

tickets.title: Enable reusable rubrics and criteria for consistent response evaluation
tickets.description: Teachers often evaluate against consistent criteria; add a way to define or select a rubric aligned to the prompt (e.g., points, criteria, common misconceptions). The evaluator should use the selected rubric to produce criterion-level scores and targeted feedback per criterion. Support saving rubrics per course/lesson and reusing them across assignments to reduce setup time. Include defaults when no rubric is provided (e.g., correct/incorrect + misconception tags) to keep the feature lightweight.

tickets.title: Add teacher interaction tracking events across Engage Agent sessions
tickets.description: Teachers want to track their behavior and strategy usage over time, which requires capturing core interaction data in a structured way. Instrument product analytics events for key actions such as opening a session, selecting a strategy, changing a strategy, adding an annotation, viewing recommendations, and applying/acknowledging a suggestion. Ensure events include stable identifiers (teacher_id, class/session_id, timestamp, strategy_id) and are privacy-safe (no student PII in event payloads). Provide a lightweight event schema and add automated validation to prevent regressions in telemetry.

tickets.title: Implement session annotations for teachers with persistent storage
tickets.description: Enable teachers to create and view annotations tied to a session (e.g., what they tried, what worked, context for decisions). Add an annotations UI entry point in the session view, supporting create/edit/delete and timestamps, with optional tagging to a selected strategy. Store annotations in the backend and load them on session revisit, with clear empty states and autosave to avoid data loss. Consider role/visibility defaults (private to teacher unless explicitly shared) and basic export/download if already supported elsewhere.

tickets.title: Persist and surface previously selected strategies per teacher/session
tickets.description: Teachers want their prior strategy selections to persist so they can continue a plan and reflect on what they used. Save the most recent strategy selection(s) for a teacher by context (session, class, or student group as applicable) and restore them when the teacher returns. In the UI, show a “Previously selected strategies” section with last-used timestamps and quick reselect, while still allowing easy change. Ensure fallback behavior when a strategy is deprecated or not available in the current context.

tickets.title: Add a teacher strategy timeline/history view to support reflection
tickets.description: To help track teacher behavior and strategies, provide a consolidated view of what strategies were selected and when, along with related annotations. Create a simple “History” panel that lists sessions chronologically, showing strategy choices, key interactions (e.g., applied suggestion), and associated notes. Include basic filters (date range, strategy type) and drill-down into a session for detail. Keep scope to an MVP read-only view powered by the tracking events and persisted selections/annotations.

tickets.title: Add an 'Explain' view that shows step-by-step analysis with a user-friendly rationale
tickets.description: Users want more detailed analysis and results, including reasoning or explainable text. Add an optional "Explain" view (e.g., a collapsible panel or toggle) that expands the agent’s output into: key findings, supporting evidence, assumptions, and how the conclusion was reached. The default response should remain concise, with the explainable content hidden until requested to avoid clutter. Ensure the explanation is written in plain language and aligns tightly to the produced result (no unrelated generic reasoning).

tickets.title: Support citations and evidence linking within detailed analysis output
tickets.description: When the agent references documents, web sources, or prior conversation turns, provide inline citations (e.g., [1], [2]) that link to the exact source snippet or message. In the "Explain" view, include an evidence section that lists sources used, timestamps/locations, and quoted excerpts where possible. If no sources are available, clearly label statements as model inference vs. user-provided inputs to improve trust and interpretability.

tickets.title: Add a settings control to choose explanation depth (brief vs. detailed)
tickets.description: Different users want different levels of detail in reasoning and explainability. Add a per-user (and optionally per-thread) setting for explanation depth: "Off", "Brief", and "Detailed". The setting should affect both the main response formatting and whether the "Explain" view auto-expands, while keeping latency and token usage in mind by generating the detailed explanation only when needed.

tickets.title: Restore student progress summary module on teacher dashboard
tickets.description: Teachers report that the previous version included a student progress summary that is missing in the current experience. Add a dashboard module that surfaces key progress indicators (e.g., completion, proficiency/score trends, recent activity) at a glance with links to drill into student- or class-level views. Ensure the module supports common teacher workflows (filter by class/period/date range) and follows existing design patterns for dashboard cards. Include basic empty/loading/error states and confirm data sources align with current tracking/analytics pipelines.

tickets.title: Restore test analysis summary view for teachers
tickets.description: Teachers want the prior “analysis summary of tests” functionality back in the product. Implement a summarized test analytics section that highlights performance distribution, commonly missed items/skills, and trends over time, with the ability to select a test and drill into item-level and student-level breakdowns. Align metric definitions with existing reporting to avoid discrepancies, and ensure permissions restrict access to authorized teachers/classes. Add UI states for no tests available and partial data scenarios.

tickets.title: Add community gallery entry point to teacher dashboard navigation
tickets.description: Teachers request easy access to the community gallery directly from the teacher dashboard. Add a prominent navigation entry (e.g., left nav item and/or dashboard quick action card) that routes to the community gallery without requiring extra clicks or context switching. Preserve teacher context (e.g., selected class) when appropriate, and ensure the gallery page has a clear way to return to the dashboard. Validate role-based access so only teachers see the teacher-oriented entry point.

tickets.title: Add “Chat about this recommendation” conversation mode on strategy results
tickets.description: Teachers need a way to ask questions and discuss the reasoning behind a strategy recommendation. Add an entry point from the strategy recommendation view (e.g., button/link) that opens a contextual chat thread seeded with the recommendation, key inputs, and constraints. The agent should answer “why this,” “what evidence,” “what if I change X,” and “how would this look in my classroom” type questions, while staying grounded in the current student/context data. Persist the chat thread with the strategy so teachers can return later without losing context.

tickets.title: Support iterative, collaborative refinement of a recommended strategy
tickets.description: Teachers want to “debate” and collaboratively adjust the strategy rather than accept a static recommendation. Add an interactive refinement flow where the teacher can propose changes (e.g., constraints, tone, time available, accommodations) and the agent returns a revised strategy with a concise changelog highlighting what changed and why. Include a “Lock constraints” area (grade level, student needs, duration, resources) so edits are explicit and auditable. Provide an “Apply updated strategy” action that updates the saved strategy version while keeping prior versions accessible.

tickets.title: Expose recommendation rationale with citations to inputs and assumptions
tickets.description: To make discussion productive, teachers need transparency into how the agent arrived at the recommendation. Add a rationale panel on the recommendation page showing: key student/context signals used, assumptions made, and how each maps to specific parts of the strategy. Where applicable, label items as “from teacher input” vs “inferred,” and allow the teacher to correct assumptions from the panel to trigger a refresh of the recommendation. Ensure rationale content is concise and avoids revealing any sensitive information beyond what the teacher provided/has access to.

tickets.title: Enable inline editing of generated content
tickets.description: Teachers need a way to quickly modify generated content rather than accepting it as-is. Add an inline editor for generated outputs (e.g., lesson plan sections, prompts, rubrics) that supports rich text where applicable and preserves structure (headings, bullet lists). Save edits as the new canonical version for the activity and clearly indicate when content has been modified from the original generation (e.g., “Edited” state). Ensure edits are persisted and reflected everywhere the content is reused or exported.

tickets.title: Add ad-hoc update flow to revise generated content with teacher instructions
tickets.description: Teachers should be able to request targeted revisions (e.g., “simplify language,” “align to 8th grade,” “add accommodations,” “shorten to 10 minutes”) without regenerating everything. Add a “Revise” action that opens a lightweight instruction input and applies changes to the selected section(s) or whole document. Provide options to choose scope (selected text/section vs entire output) and show a diff/preview before applying. Keep a history of revisions so teachers can revert to a previous version if the update is not satisfactory.

tickets.title: Support version history and restore for teacher-modified content
tickets.description: Once teachers start editing and revising content, they need safety nets to avoid losing a good version. Implement basic versioning that snapshots generated content and subsequent teacher edits/revisions, with timestamps and labels (e.g., Generated, Edited, Revised). Provide a simple UI to view prior versions and restore a selected version. Ensure version history is stored efficiently and is available across sessions/devices.

tickets.title: Add chat-based strategy selection beyond the recommended option
tickets.description: Teachers want to explore and select alternative instructional strategies instead of being limited to a single recommended strategy. Add an optional chat entry point from the strategy step that lets teachers ask for alternatives (e.g., "give me 3 other strategies"), compare them, and apply one to the current test-generation flow. The chat should be scoped to strategy discovery/selection and write the chosen strategy back into the structured workflow state (so subsequent generation uses it). Include guardrails so the chat cannot bypass required workflow steps or create tests entirely outside the guided flow.

tickets.title: Implement natural-language overrides for generation options (count, language, etc.)
tickets.description: Teachers want to customize generation parameters via simple chat instructions (e.g., number of images, translation language) rather than being constrained to fixed defaults. Add a "Customize" chat or prompt box that parses user instructions into structured generation settings (e.g., imageCount=5, targetLanguage=es) and validates them against product limits. Surface the parsed settings back to the UI for confirmation/editing before generation, and persist them with the draft so reruns remain consistent. Ensure translations/localization are applied to generated text content where supported and provide clear errors/fallbacks if a requested option is unsupported.

tickets.title: Add in-chat editing commands to revise generated content
tickets.description: Teachers want to use the chatbot not only for explanations but to directly revise generated outputs through conversation. Implement structured chat actions (e.g., “Rewrite”, “Shorten”, “Change tone”, “Align to standards”, “Replace example”, “Differentiate for level”) that apply edits to a specific selected artifact (lesson plan, worksheet, prompt, etc.). Changes should generate a new version while preserving the prior version for comparison/undo, and clearly indicate which artifact and section is being edited to avoid ambiguity. Ensure revisions update the underlying document/state used across the app, not just the chat transcript.

tickets.title: Improve discoverability of 'chat can revise output' via contextual affordances
tickets.description: This capability may be underused if teachers don’t realize the chatbot can modify content. Add contextual UI around generated content (e.g., “Edit with chat” button, inline “Ask to revise this section” affordance, and a first-run tip) that launches the chat with the selected content pre-attached. Include suggested prompts/chips like “Make it shorter”, “Add scaffolding”, and “Differentiate” to reduce prompting effort. Make the linkage explicit with a header such as “Editing: Worksheet v2” inside the chat.

tickets.title: Add version history and diff view for chat-driven revisions
tickets.description: When the chatbot revises output, teachers need confidence and control over changes. Provide versioning for artifacts edited through chat with an easy way to view diffs (before/after), restore a previous version, and name/annotate versions (e.g., “Adjusted for Grade 7”). Surface a lightweight “Undo last change” action immediately after an edit. This reduces risk and makes iterative refinement practical within a classroom workflow.

tickets.title: Add in-product Help Chat to guide teachers through next steps
tickets.description: Teachers can get confused about what to do next and want a general help chatbot inside Engage Agent. Add a persistent Help entry point (e.g., “Help” button in header or sidebar) that opens a chat panel where users can ask questions about the workflow and the current screen. The help chat should provide context-aware guidance (e.g., suggest the next action, link to the relevant UI area, or offer step-by-step instructions) and allow users to continue their work without leaving the page. Ensure it supports common intents like “What do I do next?”, “How do I…?”, and “Where can I find…?” with quick-reply suggestions.

tickets.title: Implement help chatbot knowledge sources and escalation path
tickets.description: For the help chatbot to be useful, it needs reliable product-specific answers and a fallback when it cannot resolve an issue. Implement a knowledge base ingestion pipeline (e.g., FAQs, product docs, feature descriptions, and workflow guides) and retrieval so responses are grounded in Engage Agent content. Add an escalation option such as “Contact support” or “Report an issue” that captures the user’s question, current page/context, and optional screenshot for support follow-up. Include basic safety and quality controls (e.g., cite relevant help articles when available, and acknowledge uncertainty rather than guessing).
